code_dossier.name: LoginRequest.java
code_dossier.description: Data Transfer Object (DTO) for user login requests, encapsulating login credentials including account identifier and password for authentication purposes.

data.detailed_description:
LoginRequest is a lightweight Data Transfer Object (DTO) class designed to encapsulate user login credentials in the account management system. This class serves as a contract between the client (frontend/API consumer) and the backend authentication service.

The class utilizes Lombok's @Data annotation to automatically generate boilerplate code including getters, setters, toString(), equals(), hashCode(), and a required-args constructor. This approach significantly reduces code verbosity while maintaining full JavaBean functionality.

Located in the `dto` package under `account-api` module, this class is positioned as part of the API layer, indicating it's likely used for REST API request binding. The two fields `loginAccount` and `password` represent the essential credentials required for user authentication.

The design follows the principle of separation of concerns by keeping the DTO simple and focused solely on data transport, without including any business logic or validation annotations. The naming convention `loginAccount` suggests flexibility in accepting different types of account identifiers (email, username, phone number, etc.).
responsibilities: Encapsulate user login credentials (account identifier and password) for secure data transfer
Serve as API request body binding object for login endpoints
Provide a standardized data contract between client applications and authentication services
Enable loose coupling between presentation layer and business logic layer
